title: The war in Iran has driven the transatlantic relationship further apart
subhead: French President Emmanuel Macron hosted a conference on freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz without the US, as even Washington's closest European partners, including Germany and Italy, are openly beginning to distance themselves.

title: Iran's speaker says US negotiations have progressed but far from final deal

Obsession with primaries takes hold in French politics a year out from presidential race
subhead: On both left and right, talk of primaries continues to stall amid logistical challenges, personal rivalries and deeper strategic voids that experts say traditional parties have yet to confront.

title: Louis Verret paints the drama of football in watercolor
subhead: The artist based in Aubervilliers, a suburb of Paris, paints football's masters as if they are freeze-frames, channeling pure emotion straight to the goal.

title: Why it's important to teach children to recognize birdsong
subhead: Knowing which bird is chirping or warbling is actually child's play, as long as you approach it methodically. We asked experts to guide us in this learning process. Along the way, they gave some compelling reasons to distinguish the blackbird's song from the chaffinch's tune.

title: A photographic journey into the daily lives of home care aides
subhead: Between autumn 2024 and summer 2025, photographer Vincent Gouriou followed several home health aides in Dordogne, southwest France, documenting their often difficult daily routines as they traveled to isolated homes, provided care and offered a listening ear.

subhead: The US ambassador to the European Union continues to criticize the bloc's institutions. He was summoned on April 8 after Vice President JD Vance's attacks on Brussels.

title: How the tech lobby made secrecy part of EU law on data centers
subhead: Microsoft and the tech industry lobby secured a provision from the EU to keep environmental data about the massive data centers they operate in Europe confidential, according to an investigation by the Investigate Europe consortium, in collaboration with Le Monde.
byline: Ella Joyner,  Léa Prati and  Nico Schmidt

title: Ireland, striving to become Europe's data center hub, falls victim to its own success
subhead: Despite major pressure on the power grid and the risk of rising greenhouse gas emissions, Dublin wants to keep building these facilities for tech giants and establish itself as a European leader in the sector.
